title: KKR Carve-Out Is a Recipe for Remorse
summary: One of buyout barons’ most sought-after targets is the “corporate carve-out.” This is the purchase of a poorly performing division of a sprawling conglomerate on the assumption that private equity can run it better. KKR & Co.’s grim experience surgically removing a food business from Unilever Plc should end any illusions that such deals always offer a quick buck.

title: Barrick Says Gold Miner to Buy Back Up to $3 Billion of Shares

title: UK Fintech Wise Makes US Trading Debut With Dual Listing
summary: Wise Group Plc is making its trading debut in the US with a Nasdaq listing as the financial technology firm tries to take advantage of a larger stock market and its legions of investors.

title: Keir Starmer Delivers Speech to Save His Premiership (Full Remarks)
summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer has delivered a last-ditch effort to save his premiership this morning, with a politically charged address designed to hold back calls for a change in leadership atop Britain’s governing Labour Party. During the speech, he reiterated his message from Friday that he won’t walk away and risk “plunging the country into chaos.” The prime minister also addressed questions about his leadership. “I know people are frustrated with me,” he said. “I know I have doubters, and I know I need to prove them wrong.” Starmer also vowed to put Britain “at the heart of Europe” and announced the nationalisation of British Steel. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Citi’s Manthey Says US Stock Outperformance Has Further to Run

title: Stocks Steady as Iran Impasse Lifts Oil and Yields: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks withstood rising oil prices and higher bond yields after the US and Iran failed to agree on terms to end the war in the Middle East.

title: Emerging-Market Stocks Set for Record High on Technology Rally
summary: Emerging-market equities rose, with artificial intelligence-related stocks keeping the benchmark buoyant as stalled peace talks between the US and Iran pushed up oil prices. Most developing-nation currencies weakened against the dollar.

title: RRJ, Pimco Invest in HK Developer PCPD’s $500 Million Bond Sale
summary: RRJ Capital and Pacific Investment Management Co. are among investors in a $500 million bond offering from Hong Kong billionaire Richard Li’s Pacific Century Premium Developments Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Gold Falls as Trump Rejection of Iran Offer Fans Inflation Fears
summary: Gold fell as President Donald Trump’s rejection of Iran’s latest peace offer to end the war in the Middle East fanned inflation fears.

title: EON Set to Acquire Ovo to Create UK Energy Supply Giant
summary: EON SE announced it would acquire Ovo Energy Ltd, potentially creating one of the largest energy providers in the UK, according to a statement.

title: Oil Climbs as Hormuz Stays Shut After Trump Rebuffs Iran’s Offer
summary: Oil rose after US President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s response to his latest peace proposal, prolonging the effective closure of the crucial Strait of Hormuz.

title: Rolls-Royce Lines up Rare Euro Bond as It Seeks War Buffers

title: US Trade Delegation Likely to Visit India as Pact Talks Advance
summary: A US trade delegation is likely to visit India for talks, a New Delhi official said Monday, as the two sides continue negotiations toward a bilateral agreement.

title: UK Gilts Under Pressure
summary: UK gilt yields are rising as domestic political uncertainty surrounding Prime Minister Keir Starmer's leadership compound international inflation worries over the impasse between Iran and the US.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Reliance Is Said to Plan Offering All New Shares in Jio IPO
summary: Reliance Industries Ltd. is gearing up to offer only new shares in the initial public offering of Jio Platforms Ltd., without selldowns by existing holders, according to people familiar with the matter, marking a U-turn in the preparation for what is slated to be India’s largest ever listing.

title: Saudi Arabian Oil Exports to China Set for Deep Plunge in June
summary: Saudi Arabian crude oil exports to China for loading in June are set to plunge to around 13 million to 14 million barrels, according to traders informed by the producer.

title: India-Bound Fertilizer Shipment Scrapped Over Iran Origin Risk
summary: An India-bound fertilizer shipment booked under an April tender has been scrapped after officials raised concerns over links to Iran, according to people with direct knowledge of the matter.

title: Brent Has Found an 'Uneasy Equilibrium,' StanChart Says
summary: Emily Ashford, head of energy research at Standard Chartered, says "Brent seems to have found a sort of uneasy equilibrium at the moment between $100 and $105 [a barrel]." She speaks on Bloomberg Television as US President Donald Trump rejected Iran’s latest response to his proposal to end the war in the Middle East. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: JPMorgan Hikes Kospi Bull Case Target to 10,000 on Memory Boom
summary: JPMorgan Chase & Co. raised its target for South Korean stocks for the second time in less than a month, citing improvement in the semiconductor cycle, corporate governance reforms and industrial-sector growth.

title: Iran-Linked LPG Tanker Signals Indian Ownership Through Hormuz
summary: A liquefied petroleum gas carrier which has previously ferried Iranian cargoes is moving through the Strait of Hormuz while declaring that its crew and owners are Indian, according to ship-tracking data.

title: The Bank of England's Megan Greene on Monetary Policy in a World of Supply Side Shocks | Odd Lots
summary: Ever since Covid, central banks around the world have had the same problem. They have tools that are designed to modulate demand, but so many challenges have involved the supply side of the economy. Whether we're talking about supply chain disruptions, the war in Ukraine, and now the war in Iran, these are all issues for which monetary policy is of limited value. Of course, the temptation is to "look through" these issues, recognizing the fact that these disruptions don't say much about the actual underlying state of the economy. On this episode we speak with Megan Greene, an external member of the Monetary Policy Committee at the Bank of England. We talk about the compounding effects of all these shocks (including the trade war), how she's thinking about the first- and second-order effects of each one, and why, for now, despite the underlying weakness of the UK economy, she remains squarely focused on the risks of higher inflation. (Source: Bloomberg)
